title: Diagrams are navigation aids
canonical_summary: Diagrams help users and reviewers understand how lanes, cards, contracts, retrieval, LoRA, and evaluation fit together.
source_priority_rule: Architecture docs and current diagram notes define diagram purpose.
answer_pattern: Explain diagrams as references that support understanding.
exactness_guard: Do not treat diagrams as proof that the system works.

title: Visuals need version and status labels
canonical_summary: Visual assets should state whether they are concept, draft, reference, or publication-ready.
source_priority_rule: Versioning policy and asset notes define status.
exactness_guard: Do not ship concept visuals as final without review.

title: Diagram text must be source-consistent
canonical_summary: Diagram labels should match current project terms, version status, and claim boundaries.
source_priority_rule: Current governance and contract docs outrank visual copy.
answer_pattern: Check diagram text against project docs.
exactness_guard: Do not let generated art invent project claims.

title: Asset filenames should be descriptive
canonical_summary: Visual assets should use descriptive filenames that show subject, status, and version when useful.
source_priority_rule: Repo naming docs and directory map define placement.
answer_pattern: Recommend stable filenames for repo navigation.
exactness_guard: Do not keep opaque generated filenames as canonical asset names.

title: Diagram overstates implementation
canonical_summary: A diagram can imply the system is implemented even when it is still planning; status labels should prevent that.
source_priority_rule: Project maturity docs define what can be claimed.
answer_pattern: Add planning/reference status and avoid production language.
exactness_guard: Do not let visuals bypass release gates.

title: Diagram text is illegible or wrong
canonical_summary: Generated diagrams may contain spelling, spacing, or legibility issues and need visual QA before public use.
source_priority_rule: Human visual review outranks generated appearance.
answer_pattern: Flag the issue and regenerate or manually rebuild if needed.
exactness_guard: Do not publish unreadable diagram text.

title: Visuals drift from brand/system meaning
canonical_summary: Visuals can drift if they emphasize aesthetics over the Atlas/lane/card logic.
source_priority_rule: Core philosophy and architecture docs guide visuals.
answer_pattern: Align visual hierarchy with source -> lanes -> cards -> contract -> model -> evaluation.
exactness_guard: Do not use visuals that obscure the actual system.

title: Concept diagram vs implementation diagram
user_phrasing: is this concept or actual architecture
canonical_summary: A concept diagram explains the idea; an implementation diagram should match actual code, files, and tested workflows.
answer_pattern: Label the diagram according to maturity.
exactness_guard: Do not label concept art as implementation.

title: AI-generated image vs editable diagram source
user_phrasing: should we use generated image or editable svg
canonical_summary: Generated images are useful for ideation, but final docs may need editable diagrams such as SVG, Mermaid, or draw.io.
exactness_guard: Do not rely only on raster art for technical documentation.

title: Docs outrank diagram wording
canonical_summary: If a diagram conflicts with source docs, the docs win and the diagram should be updated.
answer_pattern: State the conflict and correct the visual.
exactness_guard: Do not update strategy to match a flawed diagram.

title: Do not confuse visual clarity with research proof
user_phrasing: diagram looks good does it prove
canonical_summary: A clear diagram helps communication but does not prove the adapter works.
answer_pattern: Separate communication quality from experimental evidence.
exactness_guard: Do not treat polished visuals as validation.

title: How to answer visual asset questions
user_phrasing: how should assistant answer diagram requests
canonical_summary: Answers should identify the audience, diagram type, maturity status, key labels, and whether an editable version is needed.
answer_pattern: Use: audience -> type -> status -> labels -> format.
